architect-level-questions.signature: discuss-set SKILL.md with system-architect and UI/UX focused gray areas
architect-level-questions.description: Gray areas focus on architectural decisions and UI/UX specifics rather than implementation coding details

variable-gray-areas.signature: 4n gray areas (n >= 1) with n AskUserQuestion selection prompts
variable-gray-areas.description: Number of gray areas scales to set complexity in multiples of 4, each batch presented as one AskUserQuestion

rich-question-format.signature: Each question includes 3-5 sentence context, pros/cons table, tagged (Recommended) option
rich-question-format.description: Questions provide enough inline context for informed decisions without external research

deferred-decisions.description: Out-of-scope questions and decisions are captured in a deferred decisions file for future milestone consideration

richer-context-md.signature: CONTEXT.md with architectural vision, UI/UX specifics, deferred items summary, and decision rationale
richer-context-md.description: Enhanced CONTEXT.md output with structured sections covering all discussed dimensions

multiples-of-four.description: Total gray area count is always a multiple of 4 to match AskUserQuestion's 4-option constraint

no-coding-questions.description: Gray areas never ask about specific implementation patterns, libraries, or coding approaches -- only architecture and UX

deferred-not-lost.description: Every out-of-scope decision raised during discussion is captured in DEFERRED.md, never silently dropped

definition.scope: Major overhaul of the discuss-set skill to ask architect-level and UI/UX questions, support variable gray area counts (multiples of 4), provide richer inline context per question (pros/cons, recommendations), produce enhanced CONTEXT.md, and manage deferred decisions in a dedicated file.

tasks.description: Restructure gray area identification to focus on architecture and UI/UX
tasks.acceptance: Generated gray areas cover system architecture, data flow, integration boundaries, and UI/UX specifics

tasks.description: Implement variable gray area count (4n) with n selection prompts
tasks.acceptance: Complex sets generate 8 or 12 gray areas; simple sets generate 4

tasks.description: Enrich question format with context, pros/cons, and recommendations
tasks.acceptance: Each question includes 3-5 sentence context, pros/cons table, and (Recommended) tag

tasks.description: Add deferred decisions capture to DEFERRED.md
Enhance CONTEXT.md output with richer structured sections
tasks.acceptance: CONTEXT.md includes architectural vision, UI/UX specifics, decision rationale, and deferred items summary

acceptance: Gray areas focus on architecture and UI/UX, not coding details
Gray area count is variable (multiples of 4) based on set complexity
Each question provides rich inline context with pros/cons and recommendations
CONTEXT.md output is significantly richer than current version
